stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 20 observations with mean 81.2 % (within-trial site SD = 4.5). trial site B has 15 observations with mean 84.4 % (within-trial site SD = 6.2). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 68.0 % (within-trial site SD = 6.5). trial site D has 8 observations with mean 75.0 % (within-trial site SD = 7.0). trial site E has 20 observations with mean 84.0 % (within-trial site SD = 7.9). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.0 and 17.5 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site E: 83.9 % (shrunk from raw 84.0). Range [83.8, 84.0] as between-trial site SD varies from 7.0 to 17.5. Pooled mean: 81.0.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 81.0 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=11.7: B = 0.978.
Interval [83.8, 84.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (84.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (81.0) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
